#ClimateChange

@extremetemps #climatechange Waiting for the heat records tomorrow...
A State cold record came first.
Exceptional 12F at Mackay 14WSW IDAHO ties its State lowest June temperature on record also set at Stanley on 15 June 1923.
Note that a semi official 8F recorded Copper Basin RAWS on 1 June 1990.

external.description: Prolonged droughts attributed to climate change are making it hard for Afghans to grow other field crops and fruits, but hardy opium poppies can still thrive.
external.title: As climate change imperils Taliban’s shift from opium, impact could be felt worldwide

value.text:
The government is trying to identify crops that can grow in dry and salty conditions. Saffron and pistachio are promising alternatives. Afghanistan is appealing to other countries to supply modified seeds that are hardy enough to grow here.

www.washingtonpost.com/world/2024/0...

record.text:
Last year or so I learned of an Atmospheric River. That was bad enough. Now today I just learned a new Weather term: Derecho, meaning storm winds of at least 58 mph, moving in a straight line, as opposed to a tornado 🌪️ WOW! Mother Nature is pissed. 
#climatechange
